name: From Algeciras to Jimena de la Frontera – circular by Castellar Nuevo
name_original: Desde Algeciras a Jimena de la Frontera – circular por Castellar Nuevo
region_intro: Road cycling around Algeciras offers diverse landscapes, from coastal paths along the Strait of Gibraltar to the extensive cork oak forests of Los Alcornocales Natural Park. The region features varied terrain, including significant elevation changes and scenic vistas of the African coastline. Over 80% of Algeciras is protected natural area, providing numerous routes through unique ecosystems. This environment presents options for both moderate rides and challenging excursions. Best road cycling routes around Algeciras The most popular road cycling route is From Algeciras to Jimena de la Frontera – circular by Castellar Nuevo, a 62.8 miles (101.0 km) moderate trail that takes 4 hours 28 minutes to complete. This route offers varied terrain connecting Algeciras to inland towns. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is From Algeciras to Punta de Tarifa, a moderate 27.7 miles (44.6 km) path. This route provides coastal views along the Strait of Gibraltar. Local road cyclists also love the Embalse de Barbate - Los Alcornocales Natural Park – circular from Algeciras, a 80.5 miles (129.6 km) trail leading through cork oak forests, often completed in about 6 hours 22 minutes. Road cycling around Algeciras is defined by coastal views, cork oak forests, and significant elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to difficult ascents. The routes in Algeciras are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Algeciras's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Algeciras?
A: Road cycling around Algeciras offers a diverse range of terrain. You'll find routes with significant elevation changes, particularly when traversing the natural parks. Many routes feature mostly paved surfaces, but some may include unpaved sections. The region is characterized by coastal paths along the Strait of Gibraltar and lush cork oak forests within Los Alcornocales Natural Park.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Algeciras area?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes available around Algeciras, catering to various skill levels. These include options from moderate to difficult, with distances and elevation gains suitable for both relaxed rides and challenging excursions.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see while cycling?
A: While cycling, you can experience breathtaking vistas of the Strait of Gibraltar, often with the African coastline visible on clear days. Routes through the Natural Park of the Strait offer unique biodiversity, while those in Los Alcornocales Natural Park immerse you in extensive cork oak forests. You might also encounter highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7560342" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gibraltar Airport runway&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/341143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gibraltar Skywalk&lt;/a&gt; on routes heading towards Gibraltar.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Algeciras?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974821999/from-algeciras-to-jimena-de-la-frontera-circular-by-castellar-nuevo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Algeciras to Jimena de la Frontera – circular by Castellar Nuevo&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate route. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956810256/embalse-de-barbate-los-alcornocales-natural-park-circular-from-algeciras" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Barbate - Los Alcornocales Natural Park – circular from Algeciras&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the cork oak forests.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Algeciras?
A: Algeciras offers a favorable climate for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures. While summer can be warm, the lush forests of Los Alcornocales Natural Park offer welcome shade on some routes. Winter is typically mild, making it possible to cycle year-round, though you should check local weather conditions.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Algeciras?
A: The road cycling routes in Algeciras are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from stunning coastal views of the Strait of Gibraltar to the immersive cork oak forests, as well as the challenging ascents and rewarding scenic vistas.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in Algeciras?
A: While many routes around Algeciras feature significant elevation changes and are rated moderate to difficult, there are some easier options available. It's best to check the specific route details for distance and elevation gain to ensure it's suitable for your family's experience level. The city is also developing new bike path infrastructure to enhance cycling experiences.

Q: Can I find routes that offer coastal views?
A: Absolutely. Algeciras is strategically located where the Mediterranean Sea meets the Atlantic Ocean, offering numerous routes with spectacular coastal views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974836371/from-algeciras-to-punta-de-tarifa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Algeciras to Punta de Tarifa&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, provides extensive coastal views along the Strait of Gibraltar.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of road cycling routes in Algeciras?
A: The routes in Algeciras cater to various fitness levels, ranging from moderate to difficult. You'll find 3 easy routes, 12 moderate routes, and 5 difficult routes. Difficult routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38283722" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Gaucín – View of Casares loop from Algeciras&lt;/a&gt;, can involve over 2000 meters of elevation gain, requiring good to very good fitness.

Q: Are there any specific natural parks that road cyclists can explore?
A: Yes, Algeciras is an excellent base for exploring two significant natural parks. The Natural Park of the Strait offers coastal routes with views of Africa, while Los Alcornocales Natural Park, one of Europe's last cork oak forests, provides extensive forests and rich biodiversity. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10283307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellar Nuevo de la Frontera – Jimena de la Frontera loop from Algeciras&lt;/a&gt; will take you through these protected areas.

Q: Where can I find information about parking or public transport for accessing routes?
A: While specific parking and public transport details for individual trailheads are not provided here, Algeciras serves as a convenient starting point for many routes. For general information about the region and its accessibility, you might find resources on local tourism sites like &lt;a href="https://visitingcadiz.com/algeciras-gateway-to-the-mediterranean-and-historic-heart-of-the-campo-de-gibraltar/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Visiting Cadiz&lt;/a&gt;, which can offer insights into reaching the area.
tips: Center of Algeciras where the most important festivals of the city are celebrated. It is recommended to see the ceramic benches with motifs based on "Don Quixote" by Cervantes.
One of the few examples of modern architecture in the town. According to the author, its spiral shape represents a sea shell.
A good bike lane between San Roque Station and Almoraima, 7 km long and is part of EuroVelo 8 (Mediterranean route)

Castellar "El Nuevo" is a modern part of Castellar de la Frontera, inaugurated in 1971. The most interesting places in the town are: the Divino Salvador Church with the image of the Santísimo Cristo de la Almoraima and the Plaza Andalucía with a sculpture that commemorates the XXV Anniversary of the creation of the new town and also another sculpture dedicated to Human Rights.

waypoints.name: EuroVelo 8 Cycle Path: San Roque to Castellar
